content: I didn't find it in zulip search or mailing list.. Is there a repository that ports miscdev and file::operations on top of the rust-next branch?

Gwan-gyeong Mun said:

I didn't find it in zulip search or mailing list.. Is there a repository that ports miscdev and file::operations on top of the rust-next branch?

I don't think miscdev, but @Wedson Almeida Filho and @Ariel Miculas is working with read only file operations I think.

Andreas Hindborg 말함:

Gwan-gyeong Mun said:

I didn't find it in zulip search or mailing list.. Is there a repository that ports miscdev and file::operations on top of the rust-next branch?

I don't think miscdev, but Wedson Almeida Filho and Ariel Miculas is working with read only file operations I think.

Thanks for the info!

Wedson's fs branch implements file::operations, you can find a recently rebased (on rust-next) version of this branch here. Although there are no plans for upstreaming these filesystem abstractions yet (there needs to be a user for them first).
Wedson is planning to upstream his vfs branch this week, which contains read-only filesystem abstractions necessary for his rtarfs driver and for my puzzlefs driver.
Binder has abstractions to define the types that appear in many of the file operations methods, but it does not actually define the operations trait itself.
btw, what's the status of the Various Rust bindings for files patch?
I never really got any review for it, but there's a newer version in the Rust binder branch.
